title: NaturalDivergence
summary: Packages natural divergences and the cone-rule interface used to convert ambient identities into PE divergence statements.

mathematicalContent.heading: Mathematical role
mathematicalContent.body: Packages natural divergences and the cone-rule interface used to convert ambient identities into PE divergence statements.

mathematicalContent.heading: How to read the translation
mathematicalContent.body: The correspondence below follows Ambient/PE/Invariants/NaturalDivergence.lean declaration by declaration. A definition is translated by its defining expression, a theorem by its quantified proposition, and a structure, class, or inductive type by the mathematical data encoded in its fields or constructors. The original Lean signature remains visible beside every translation.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: A scalar invariant represented as the divergence of a rank-one invariant. In explicit terms, for i of type scalar invariant, its defining expression is exists F : One Form Invariant, I equals div Invariant F.

A natural divergence whose one-form has a prescribed lower weight bound. In explicit terms, for i of type scalar invariant, and lowerweight in the integers, its defining expression is exists F : One Form Invariant, I equals div Invariant F and lower Weight < equals F.1.weight.
For I of type Scalar Invariant, lowerWeight in the integers, and h of type Natural Divergence At Weight I lower Weight, Natural Divergence I.
correspondence.leanType: theorem NaturalDivergenceAtWeight.toNaturalDivergence {I : ScalarInvariant} {lowerWeight : Int} (h : NaturalDivergenceAtWeight I lowerWeight) : NaturalDivergence I

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Dilation-field annihilation of the last ambient tensor index. In explicit terms, for i of type ambient invariant, its defining expression is Invariant Evaluation Model.XAnnihilates Last Index I.

The scalar ambient expression occurring in Proposition 3.10. In explicit terms, for n, k in the natural numbers, and ttilde of type ambient one form invariant, its defining expression is restrict To Einstein Slice ambient Laplacian Iter n divided by 2 minus k minus 1 ambient Div Invariant Ttilde.
correspondence.leanType: def proposition310Invariant (n k : Nat) (Ttilde : AmbientOneFormInvariant) : ScalarInvariant

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Semantic interface for the cone-decomposition argument in Proposition 3.10. Its eventual instance will use AmbientPEBridge.ambient_metric_cone_decomposition. Its mathematical data are: proposition310: for every n k Ttilde : Ambient One Form Invariant, Even n maps to Ttilde.1.weight equals -2 times k : Int maps to 2 minus n : Int < equals Ttilde.1.weight maps to XAnnihilates Last Index Ttilde.1 maps to Natural Divergence proposition310 Invariant n k Ttilde.
Proposition 3.10 as exposed by the semantic cone-decomposition rule. In symbols, the proposition is the statement displayed below.
correspondence.leanType: theorem ambient_rank_one_divergence_is_natural_divergence [InvariantEvaluationModel] [AmbientRankOneDivergenceRule] (n k : Nat) (Ttilde : AmbientOneFormInvariant) (hn : Even n) (hWeight : Ttilde.1.weight = -2 * (k : Int)) (hBound : 2 - (n : Int) <= Ttilde.1.weight) (hX : XAnnihilatesLastIndex Ttilde.1) : NaturalDivergence (proposition310Invariant n k Ttilde)

references.locator: Proposition 3.7, Proposition 3.10, Lemma 4.1, and Theorems 1.1/1.4 as applicable
references.note: Primary source for the renormalized-curvature-integral mechanism represented by these modules.

limitations: The displayed Lean results are checked relative to structures, typeclasses, certificates, or model laws supplied as hypotheses. The module does not by itself derive every interface field from a concrete geometric backend.
